nodes.content: The speaker informally pitches their startup, Banana AI, described as a frontier phone agentic AI software with over 200 users and a memory layer for phone calls, to someone claiming to be a VC at Andreessen Horowitz (a16z). The VC expresses strong interest in investing and offers to be contacted when the startup is ready for investment, providing an email address (a.green@bob.com). The VC also offers mentorship support via a contact named Hannah Montana, providing her email (hanna.montana@waterbottle.com) and encouraging the founder to reach out, mentioning that they were referred by the VC.

action_items: Email VC at agreen@bob.com about Banana AI investment
Email Hannah Montana at hanna.montana@waterbottle.com for startup mentorship

apps_results.content:
## Startup overview
- Startup name: banana.ai (phone agentic AI)
- Acts as memory layer for phone calls
- Positioned as frontier phone agent software

## Traction and funding
- Currently has over 200 users
- Actively seeking investment funding
- Open to further discussions with interested investors

## Investor interest and contact
- VC from a16z expresses strong interest
- Primary contact email: green@bob.com
- Invitation to email when ready for investments

## Mentorship and advisor contact
- Mentor recommended: Hannah Montana
- Hannah described as very experienced in field
- Hannah’s email: hanna.montana@waterbottle.com

## Next steps
- Founder to email green@bob.com for investment talks
- Founder to email Hannah Montana for mentorship
- Prepare more detailed materials before outreach

A reporter from ABC News, Michael Lee, informally interviews Wendy, a Harvard University student majoring in computer science and philosophy. Wendy explains she chose computer science for its high earning potential, citing big tech companies and startup culture. She mentions recently winning multiple awards (grand prize, most technical, most viral) at the ARA ARA hackathon. When asked how prospective CS students can achieve similar success, she emphasizes being able to "vibe code" and network. For networking or follow-up questions, she provides her email (Wendy.Harvard@treehacks.com) but notes she does not have a phone. She advises students to search for resources on Google. When asked for steps to be as successful as her, she responds that one is either born with it or not, and the interview ends.

apps_results.content:
## Wendy’s Background  
- Wendy studies at small “Harvard University” community college  
- Majors: computer science and philosophy  
- Interview conducted by ABC News reporter Michael Lee  

## Motivation for Studying CS  
- Chose CS mainly because “it makes a lot of money”  
- Believes big tech companies earn a lot  
- Interested in CS startup and hackathon culture  

## Hackathon Achievements  
- Recently attended ARA ARA hackathon  
- Team won grand prize  
- Also won most technical and most viral prizes  

## Advice for Prospective CS Students  
- Key skills: “vibe code” and network well  
- For resources, Wendy suggests just “search it up on Google”  
- Mentions “you can use Oh” as an additional tool/source  

## Contact and Attitude on Success  
- Contact email: Wendy.Harvard@treehacks.com  
- Says she does not have a phone  
- Believes success is innate: “born with it or you’re not”  

## Next Steps  
- Prospective students can email Wendy with questions  
- Students should practice coding, networking, and using online resources

Several people introduce themselves in a casual setting. Chelsea, founder of Banana.ai, speaks with another founder of Lettuce.ai, who describes offering 'Lettuce as a service' and mentions being a molecular biology PhD from Cornell. Chelsea also identifies herself as a biomedical engineering major at Johns Hopkins. Another participant briefly comments that their idea might be questionable and asks to pass on sharing more, and the group reassures them there is no pressure.

nodes.content: Chelsea and Wendy meet near Hopkins, introduce themselves, and realize they both attend the same school. Chelsea mentions she is building a startup called Banana.ai and recently raised $500K in funding. Wendy expresses interest in startups and asks for help finding funding connections. Chelsea recommends a contact named Nathan, describes him as intimidating, and provides his email address (nathan@waterproof.com) as a potential resource.

action_items: Email Nathan at nathan@waterproof.com about startup funding advice
